title: phaseolus vulgaris var. rachelianus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. rachelianus, also known as Jacob's Cattle Bean or Rachel's Bean, is a rare variety of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris), identified by its distinctive white and red coloration. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to its high content of polyphenols and anthocyanins. The health benefits of consuming Phaseolus vulgaris var. rachelianus include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and antioxidant protection, making it a potentially valuable addition to a healthy diet. In herbal preparations, the bean is typically dried and ground into a powder for use in teas, infusions, and other remedies, although more research is needed to fully explore its potential uses.
